# Threshold Models of Collective Behavior

**Authors:** Mark Granovetter  
**Year:** 1978  
**URL:** https://sociology.stanford.edu/publications/threshold-models-collective-behavior  
**Evidence type:** Mathematical sociology model

## Claim supported

Collective outcomes can change sharply when visible participation crosses individual thresholds.

## Key result

Threshold distributions determine equilibrium participation; similar average preferences can produce different aggregate outcomes.

## How it supports manufactured traction

This source strengthens the argument that digital systems can be moved by manipulated proxy signals. Use it as evidence for the mechanism, not as proof that every campaign succeeds.
